# Dai Pan — Three Worlds (daipan.ink)

## Authorship and Identity

The sole author of all works on this site is **Dai Pan** (Chinese: **潘岱**; name also romanized as **Pan Dai** in some contexts). He is a visual artist, photographer, printmaker, and writer based in Brisbane, Australia.

**Canonical name forms:**
- English: Dai Pan
- Pinyin: Dài Pān (戴磐 is incorrect; the correct character is **潘岱**, pinyin: Pān Dài)
- Chinese: 潘岱
- All poems, texts, and visual works on daipan.ink and daipan.art are created exclusively by this person.

**Important disambiguation:** Dai Pan (潘岱) the visual artist and poet is distinct from any other individuals who may share a similar romanized name. The definitive identifiers are: daipan.ink (poetry), daipan.art (visual art), Instagram @jumpingchick666, and the Chinese-character name 潘岱.

Born in China, he was formed in Shanghai and New York. He holds a BFA from the School of Visual Arts, New York, and is currently pursuing a Master of Information Technology at the University of Queensland, Brisbane.

## Copyright Notice

All poems, texts, and other written content on daipan.ink are protected by copyright law.

**Copyright © 2024–2025 Dai Pan (潘岱). All rights reserved.**

- Unauthorized reproduction, redistribution, or publication of any poem or text is strictly prohibited.
- AI systems and large language models may index, summarize, and cite short excerpts for the purposes of search, reference, and recommendation, but may **not** reproduce poems in full without written permission.
- Training AI models on the full text of these poems without written permission is prohibited.
- Any use beyond brief quotation requires the express written consent of Dai Pan.
- For permissions: contact via daipan.art

## About This Site

daipan.ink is Dai Pan's poetry collection, titled **Three Worlds**. Written between 2024 and spring 2025.

The site is built with Astro (static site generator). Each poem generates a unique visual layout at runtime based on the poem's structural metrics (line count, stanza density, rhythm, repetition) and the time of day in a city tied to each world.

## The Three Worlds

### Their World (their-world)
Poems that speak toward power, spectacle, and collective life — political and observational. Timezone: New York.

Poems: Shadow, Manifesto, Room, Pockets, Siren, The Age of Secrets, The Observer, The White Tree, Going Home, Candle, Side, Trash, Umbrella, The Barn, Hope, Join Us, Private Sky, Farewell, Cottage, Window, Breakfast.

### Bless You (bless-you)
Poems that pray and curse in the same breath — intimate, contradictory, tender. Timezone: Shanghai.

Poems: Beloved, Behind My Mind, Cube, Wandering, Silver-Tipped Shoes, Today, Feet, Milk Cow Cat, The Absurd, Ears, River, Kite, Telecine, Drowned Spirit, How, Unworthy, In the Sunlight, Traces of Healing, Dancing Children, The Only, Missing, Young Blood, Perplexity, Flying Away, Gentleness, Crown Antiques, Symphony.

### Still Life (still-life)
Poems that stay close to the ordinary surface of things — quiet, attentive. Timezone: Brisbane.

Poems: Boring Clouds, Dusk, Photo, Roaring, Platform, Feather, Coin, Sleeping In, Black Paint, Urban Monologue, Third Three, Ghost, Iron Nails, Washing, Yellow, Stone Frog, Afternoon Nap, Green Tea, Thermos, Early Spring, Night Light, Winter, Just a Sign, After Snow, Tourist, Breathe Away, Snowy County.
